Empires & Puzzles El Duque Review: The Furious Gargoyle Fist

El Duque was once the speed demon of the Gargoyles, bringing infinite defense down stacks at blistering Very Fast speed. But in an era where modern heroes have double his health, is his Stoneskin enough to keep him alive, or is he just a fragile relic of the past? We analyze his niche viability.
El Duque: The Furious Gargoyle Fist of Speed

El Duque is a classic Legendary hero from the Sanctuary of Gargoyles event, originally released in May 2022. As a Fire element Monk, he was designed as a high-velocity damage dealer whose primary gimmick is an accumulating defense down stack. He operates at Very Fast speed, which was his main selling point upon release, allowing him to fire repeatedly and build up debilitating debuffs on the enemy team. Unlike many modern heroes who do everything at once, El Duque has a singular, focused purpose: hit fast and make the enemy softer with every punch. He looks like he skipped leg day to focus entirely on his right hook.

El Duque presents a classic gargoyle aesthetic but with a more demonic, brawler twist compared to his stony brethren. He features large horns, wings, and a hulking grey muscular form, accented with dark red armor plates that suggest a gladiatorial background. The visual focus is undeniably on his massive, glowing fist, which visually communicates his special skill perfectly. The art style is gritty and fits the slightly darker, gothic theme of the Gargoyle event, standing apart from the more high-fantasy designs of the standard roster.
El Duque's stats reflect the era of his release, and unfortunately, time has not been kind to him.
When we compare El Duque to the heroes of late 2025 and 2026, the power creep is stark.
His Limit Broken (LB2) attack of ~1026 is now lower than many modern
heroes (e.g., Hotspin sits at 1027). Modern 5-star attackers regularly exceed 1600 attack.
His survivability is critically low by modern standards. With ~1800 HP at maximum investment, he has nearly 1000 less HP than current legendary heroes, who push past 2800.
. He relies entirely on his Stoneskin passive and speed to survive, as he cannot take a direct hit from a modern sniper without crumbling.

The -8% (or -9% after balance updates) defense reduction is a
, not a status ailment. This means it bypasses immunities to defense down ailments (though not stack immunities) and, crucially, it stacks with

It takes time. To reach a meaningful -40% or -50% defense down, you need to fire him 5 or 6 times. In a raid, the battle is usually decided long before then.
The damage percentage (355% to target) is respectable for Very Fast speed, but his low base attack stat means the actual output is underwhelming against modern defense stats.

Hitting 3 targets at Very Fast is efficient, but don't expect one-shot kills. He is a chipper, wearing teams down over time.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
El Duque relies heavily on his passive to function as a glass cannon.
The Gargoyle family bonus provides resistance to Poison, Burn, and Water damage type status ailments.

Immunity to Poison, Burn, and Water damage.
This is a situational bonus. While immunity to burn is excellent against the ubiquitous Fire meta heroes, it is a defensive perk on an offensive hero.
This is the MVP of his kit. Every time he fires, his skin hardens, reducing the damage of the next

Because he is Very Fast, he can theoretically keep this Stoneskin active almost permanently if you can ghost tiles or feed him mana. This allows him to tank hits from heroes that deal 2000+ damage, taking only 1 damage instead. It is the only reason he is playable in the current era. Note that it only blocks direct damage, not DoT or status effects.
A generic and frankly disappointing choice for him. Since his goal is to
take damage (via Stoneskin) or avoid being hit, counterattack has negative synergy with his Stoneskin passive (if you take 1 damage, you counterattack for essentially nothing).

Upon release, El Duque was hyped as a Titan killer. Players theorized that his -90% defense stack would allow for record-breaking scores. However, practical testing revealed that building stacks takes too long for daily titans. He found a niche in "Rush" wars and as a flank who could punish bad boards.

hero. He is not viable for top-tier defense teams due to his paper-thin stats. On offense, he can still be fun in a red stack, specifically because his Very Fast speed allows him to abuse the Stoneskin mechanic to survive against much stronger enemies. He is largely a "Bench" hero for competitive play but remains useful for Mythic Titans or Tower events where battles drag on.
El Duque currently does not have a costume.

El Duque is a Monk. He needs survivability to stay alive long enough to fire, but his role is damage. However, given his base stats are so low, adding defense nodes yields diminishing returns against modern snipers who will overkill him anyway.
Maximizes his damage output, which is his primary job. He relies on Stoneskin for survival, not his HP bar.
If Stoneskin is down, he dies to a stiff breeze.
Might survive a few slash attacks or minion hits.
His special skill hits like a wet noodle.

His health and defense are shockingly low for the modern game.
Reaching the maximum potential of his stack takes too long for most PvP matches.

The longer battle duration allows him to reach high stack counts (-50% to -90% defense), significantly boosting team damage.
In Impossible floors where enemies have massive HP, his stacking defense down helps whittle them down.
Against slow teams, he can fire multiple times before they retaliate, keeping Stoneskin up and crippling their defense.
El Duque works best when he can fire repeatedly without interruption.

Do not pair him with heroes that override his defense down, though his is a stack so it rarely conflicts. His main anti-synergy is with passive playstyles; he needs to be fed tiles constantly.

If you pulled El Duque early in your career, he is a fantastic asset. His speed allows him to punch above his weight class, and Stoneskin will save you from bosses that should one-shot you. Ascend him, but be aware he will eventually become too fragile for the highest levels of play.

El Duque is likely a luxury toy for you. Unless you are chasing high scores on the Red Mythic Titan, he probably doesn't deserve Alpha Aethers or Limit Break 2 materials. There are simply too many modern Reds with better stats and immediate impact. Keep him at 4/80 or 4/85 for depth in War cleanups.
The community has had a love-hate relationship with El Duque since day one.

Renowned player JekylandHyde famously debunked the "El Duque is the Titan King" myth. He tested El Duque on daily titans and found that the time and battle items required to build the stack to -90% resulted in
overall scores than using a standard defense down hero, simply because you wasted 60 seconds of the fight just firing his special.
Players initially called him the "most balanced" of the Gargoyles, which in Empires & Puzzles speak often means "underpowered." While heroes like
defined the meta, El Duque was seen as "solid but skippable."

El Duque is a relic of a different time—a time when -8% defense down stacks felt revolutionary and 800 defense felt sturdy. In the modern era of 1300 defense behemoths, he is a fragile glass cannon who relies entirely on his gargoyle skin to survive. He isn't the meta-breaker he was once feared to be, but there is still a visceral satisfaction in firing his special three times in a row and watching the enemy crumble. If you have him, use him for the fun factor, but don't expect him to carry your defense team.
A niche specialist for long boss fights and a fun offensive option, but largely retired from serious competitive duty.
Is El Duque still worth ascending in 2026? A detailed review of the Very Fast Gargoyle hero, analyzing his stacking defense down, outdated stats, and niche viability in the modern meta.
